novel_features.name: Local history reconstruction
novel_features.description: Reconstruct historical FX rates from your prior `latest` syncs — free tier gets time travel without `/history` quota.
novel_features.rationale: Requires append-only `rates_snapshots` table populated from every `latest` call; no competing CLI persists snapshots.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when the user asks for past rates and the API key is on the Free or Standard tier where `/history` returns plan-upgrade-required.

novel_features.name: Watchlist with drift alerts
novel_features.description: Define currency pairs with movement thresholds; one command reports which crossed since the last check.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a persistent `watchlist` table joined against `rates_snapshots`; no other CLI has a watchlist concept.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use as a periodic monitor (cron/agent loop) instead of polling a paid alerting service.

novel_features.name: Drift detection between syncs
novel_features.description: Diff the latest snapshot against any prior point and report the biggest movers as ranked output.
novel_features.rationale: Joins two rows of `rates_snapshots`; requires local history nobody else stores.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to surface unusual FX moves the user cares about without writing a custom analysis.

novel_features.name: Quota burn projection
novel_features.description: Fit a trend over `quota_snapshots` and project when your quota will exhaust before refresh day.
novel_features.rationale: Requires longitudinal quota history; the single-call `/quota` endpoint cannot do this.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use before deploying anything that will hammer the API — the projection tells you if you'll survive the month.

novel_features.name: N×N cross-rate matrix
novel_features.description: Show full cross-rate matrix for any set of currencies from a single `latest` call — N² rates, 1 quota tick.
novel_features.rationale: Computes cross-rates locally from one base; no other tool does this from one API call.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when comparing many pairs at once; saves quota and runs offline after one sync.

novel_features.name: Batch conversion from stdin
novel_features.description: Pipe a list of amounts to a single command; converts all of them with one rate fetch.
novel_features.rationale: Standard `convert` calls the API per invocation; batching with one fetch is unique here.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when processing many amounts (orders, transactions) without burning the quota linearly.

novel_features.name: Plan/tier probe
novel_features.description: Probe each tier-gated endpoint with a single low-cost request; reports which tier your key supports.
novel_features.rationale: No competitor probes which tier the user is on; everyone reads pricing pages by hand.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use right after `doctor` to know which commands will work without `plan-upgrade-required`.

novel_features.name: Time-traveling cache reads
novel_features.description: Look up the rate from local snapshots at any historical timestamp — free tier time travel.
novel_features.rationale: Reads `rates_snapshots` for the closest captured rate at a timestamp; no other CLI captures snapshots.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when reconstructing the rate that was live at a specific moment without needing the paid `/history` endpoint.

novel_features.name: Conversion log
novel_features.description: Every `convert` call is logged; query the log by base, target, time window for recurring conversion analysis.
novel_features.rationale: Persistent log isn't an API feature; only a local store can offer it.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to remind an agent what FX work was done recently without re-querying the API.

novel_features.name: MCP server for agents
novel_features.description: Every user-facing command is exposed as an MCP tool with read-only annotations on safe queries.
novel_features.rationale: Generator emits the cobratree mirror; no existing ExchangeRate-API MCP server exists.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when wiring Claude/Cursor/Windsurf to FX context — no competing MCP for this API today.

narrative.headline: Every ExchangeRate-API endpoint, plus a local rate history, watchlists, drift alerts, and an MCP server no one else ships.
narrative.value_prop: This CLI exposes every ExchangeRate-API endpoint (latest, pair, codes, quota, enriched, history) as typed commands and layers on offline-first features the API can't provide: local snapshots in SQLite, watchlists with drift alerts, quota burn projection, N×N cross-rate matrices from a single API call, and an MCP server for AI agents. Free-tier users get time travel from their own captured history without paying for /history access.
narrative.auth_narrative: Set `EXCHANGERATE_API_KEY` from your ExchangeRate-API dashboard (https://app.exchangerate-api.com/). All commands route through `https://v6.exchangerate-api.com/v6/{key}/...`. The `open` command uses the keyless `https://open.er-api.com/v6/latest/{base}` endpoint and requires the 'Rates By Exchange Rate API' attribution if you redistribute its output.

quickstart.comment: Confirms key works and the API is reachable
Pulls latest rates and seeds local snapshots; foundation for offline + history features
Single-pair lookup, the most common request
Multi-target conversion with one API call
Cross-rate matrix — N² rates, 1 quota tick
How fast you're burning the monthly 1500-call quota

troubleshoots.fix: Run `exchangerate-api-pp-cli doctor` to confirm `EXCHANGERATE_API_KEY` is exported in the current shell.
Hit your monthly limit. Use cached data via `pair --as-of`, `history-cache`, or wait until your refresh day (see `quota` output).
Those endpoints require a paid tier. Use `plan-check` to see which endpoints your key supports.
Fall back to local snapshots: `history-cache <BASE> <TARGET>` if you've been syncing.
Open-access endpoint resets after 20 minutes. Switch to a keyed `latest <BASE>` call.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for exchangerate-api-pp-cli whenever an agent or script needs live FX rates, multi-target conversions, or persistent historical context. It's the right pick over a one-off HTTP call when the agent will run more than once on the same data, when quota matters (1500/mo Free tier), or when the workflow benefits from a watchlist, drift report, or cross-rate matrix that requires local state. Use the MCP server (`mcp serve`) when wiring FX context into Claude Desktop, Cursor, or Windsurf.

recipes.title: One-shot conversion in a script
recipes.explanation: Returns just the converted number for downstream piping.

recipes.title: Daily sync + drift report (cron)
recipes.command: exchangerate-api-pp-cli sync-rates --base USD && exchangerate-api-pp-cli drift --base USD --since 24h --top 10 --json
recipes.explanation: Captures today's rates and surfaces the largest movers vs the prior snapshot.

recipes.title: Pre-deploy quota check
recipes.explanation: Projects when the monthly quota will run out, given recent usage.

recipes.title: Cross-rate matrix for a portfolio
recipes.explanation: Full N×N matrix from one API call; pipes cleanly into spreadsheets.

recipes.title: Watch a pair and alert when it moves
recipes.command: exchangerate-api-pp-cli watch add USD EUR --threshold 1.0 && exchangerate-api-pp-cli watch check --json
recipes.explanation: Stores a threshold; subsequent `watch check` calls flag any pair that crossed.
